'''Joe Bullock''' (born 27 November 1992) is an English professional [[rugby league]] footballer who plays as a {{rlp|pr}} forward for the [[Barrow Raiders]] in the [[RFL Championship]].

Bullock has spent time on loan from [[Wigan Warriors|Wigan]] at the [[South Wales Scorpions]] in [[2012 Championship 1|Championship 1]]. Bullock played for the [[Leigh Centurions]] in the [[RFL Championship|Championship]], and on loan from [[Leigh Centurions|Leigh]] at [[South Wales Scorpions|South Wales]] in [[2013 Championship 1|Championship 1]], and the [[Barrow Raiders]] in the [[2014 RFL Championship|Championship]]. He joined [[Barrow Raiders|Barrow]] on a permanent deal in  [[2015 Championship 1|Championship 1]], [[League 1 (rugby league)|League 1]] and [[2018 RFL Championship|Betfred Championship]].

==Background==
Bullock was born in [[Blackpool]], [[Lancashire]], England.

==Playing career==
===Wigan===
He began his career as a winger for Blackpool Scorpions ARLFC before being scouted by Wigan whilst still a junior. In 2019, he made his Super League début for [[Wigan Warriors|Wigan]] against [[St Helens R.F.C.|St Helens]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/match/EVP2921664|title=Super League: St Helens v Wigan Warriors|publisher=BBC}}</ref>
Bullock played in the [[2020 Super League Grand Final]] which Wigan lost 8-4 against St Helens.<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/55072997|title=Super League Grand Final: Wigan 4-8 St Helens|work=BBC Sport }}</ref>

===Warrington===
In 2022, Bullock signed for Warrington. In his first season at the club he played 24 matches as the Warrington side finished 11th on the table and narrowly avoided relegation.
Bullock played 17 games for Warrington in the 2023 Super League season as Warrington finished sixth on the table and qualified for the playoffs. He played in the clubs elimination playoff loss against St Helens.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.warringtonguardian.co.uk/sport/23827325.highs-lows-warrington-wolves-2023-season/|title=Highs and lows from Warrington Wolves' 2023 season|website=www.warringtonguardian.co.uk|date=3 October 2023 }}</ref>

===Widnes (DR loan)===
On 18 February 2024, it was reported that he had signed for [[Widnes Vikings|Widnes]] in the [[RFL Championship]] on DR loan.<ref>{{cite web|website=Warrington Guardian|date=18 Feb 2024|author=Matt Turner|title=Warrington Wolves players feature in 1895 Cup clash|url=https://www.warringtonguardian.co.uk/sport/wolves/wolvesnews/24127461.warrington-wolves-players-feature-1895-cup-clash/}}</ref>

===Hull F.C. (loan)===
On 27 February 2024, it was reported that he had signed for [[Hull F.C.]] in the Super League on short-term loan.<ref>{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League|date=27 Feb 2024|author=Aaron Bower|title=Joe Bullock loan destination revealed as Warrington Wolves prop joins Super League rivals|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/joe-bullock-loan-destination-revealed-as-warrington-wolves-prop-joins-super-league-rivals}}</ref>

===Salford (loan)===
On 16 Jul 2024 it was reported that he had signed for [[Salford Red Devils|Salford]] in the Super League on season-long loan<ref>{{cite web|website=BBC Sport|date=16 Jul 2024|accessdate=17 Jul 2024|title=Salford sign Warrington prop Bullock on loan|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/articles/c3g9777g9wko}}</ref>

===Salford===
On 23 October 2024, it was reported that he had signed for Salford in the Super League on a two-year deal, making his earlier loan move permanent.<ref>{{cite web|website=Yorkshire Live|date=23 Oct 2024|author=Matthew Shaw|title=Salford Red Devils land Warrington Wolves forward as pack revamp continues|url=https://www.examinerlive.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/salford-red-devils-warrington-wolves-30215287}}</ref>

===Barrow Raiders (loan)===
On 9 April 2025 it was reported that he had signed for [[Barrow Raiders]] in the [[RFL Championship]] on a short-term loan<ref>{{cite web|website=Total RL|date=9 Apr 2025|accessdate=16 Apr 2025|author=Callum Walker|title=Salford Red Devils prop Joe Bullock makes curious short-term loan move|url=https://www.totalrl.com/salford-red-devils-prop-joe-bullock-makes-curious-short-term-loan-move/}}</ref>

On 26 June 2025 it was reported that he had re-joined [[Barrow Raiders]] for the second time this season on DR loan<ref>{{cite web|website=Total RL|date=26 Jun 2025|author=Stephen Ibbetson|title=Salford Red Devils prop to feature for Barrow Raiders|url=https://www.totalrl.com/salford-red-devils-prop-to-feature-for-barrow-raiders/}}</ref>

===Barrow Raiders===
On 13 August 2025 it was reported that he has turned his loan move into a permanent move and signed for [[Barrow Raiders]] in the [[RFL Championship]] on a 2-year deal.<ref>{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League|date=13 Aug 2025|author=Ben Olawumi|title=Veteran prop makes permanent Salford Red Devils exit as Championship deal agreed|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/veteran-prop-makes-permanent-salford-red-devils-exit-as-championship-deal-agreed}}</ref>
